authors: Silin Chen, Shaoxin Lin, Yuling Shi, Heng Lian, Xiaodong Gu, Longfei Yun, Dong Chen, Lin Cao, Jiyang Liu, Nu Xia, Qianxiang Wang
title: SWE-Exp: Experience-Driven Software Issue Resolution
storage_format: Multi-facet Experience Bank in a vector database (Multilingual-E5-Large embeddings): each experience is a dictionary with issue_type (descriptive label, e.g. VariableReferenceError), description (conditions and scenarios), and perspective or modification (abstract insights). Comprehension experiences guide diagnosis (problem perspective); modification experiences guide safe code editing (repair strategies). Offline extraction via the Experiencer agent from successful and failed trajectories.
performance_metrics: Pass@1 (%), Average Token Costs, Average API Costs ($), Average Wall Time
baseline_compared: Agentless, SWE-Agent, SWE-Search, OpenHands, EvoCoder, SAGE
memory_size_over_time: Fixed after offline construction of the experience bank; gain saturation at ~300 experiences (Figure 4b); performance plateaus with 1-2pp variation beyond that point
main_result: SWE-Exp achieves 73.0% Pass@1 with Claude 4 Sonnet and 42.0% with DeepSeek-V3-0324 on SWE-bench Verified, surpassing all baselines. Comprehension and modification experiences contribute independently (+3.2% and +2.6%); experience extraction is the most critical component (-6.0% without it).
performance_vs_baseline: +2.2pp vs SWE-Search with Claude 4 Sonnet (73.0% vs 70.8%); +6.6pp vs SWE-Search with DeepSeek (42.0% vs 35.4%); +3.2pp vs SWE-Agent with DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.8%); +4.2pp vs EvoCoder with DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.0%)
cost_vs_baseline: $0.13 vs $0.12 per issue with DeepSeek (+8.3%); wall time 15min49s vs 12min37s (+25%); retrieval+rerank overhead 37.5s avg
limitations: Effectiveness depends on the quality of extracted experiences; k>1 experiences degrade performance (42.0%→39.0% with k=4); evaluation only on Python projects; the Instructor may become over-dependent on experiences even when direct exploration would suffice
validity_threats: Single model per configuration (DeepSeek or Claude 4 Sonnet); SWE-bench may overlap with the LLM's training data; temporal leakage mitigated but residual (excludes same-repo + post-target); evaluation only on Python; experience quality assessed on a subset of 75 instances
